metadata.name: ww-input-radio
metadata.description: A form radio button group component that allows users to select a single option from a list of choices. Supports both static and dynamic data sources with configurable label and value fields, flexible layout direction (vertical/horizontal), and responsive styling options.
features: Single selection from a list of radio button options
Static choices with label/value pairs or dynamic data binding from collections
Configurable label and value fields for dynamic object data
Responsive gap, alignment, and justify content controls
Flex wrap support for horizontal layout
Does not support multi-select (use ww-input-checkbox for that)
Does not support disabled state per option
Does not support grouping or nested options

properties.name: options
properties.description: Array of choices for the radio group. Each item can be a simple string/number or an object with label and value fields. When bound to a collection of objects, use labelField and valueField to specify which properties to use.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The initial selected value of the radio group. Should match one of the option values.

properties.name: labelField
properties.description: Object property path to use as the label when options are bound to a collection of objects. Only relevant when options is a bound array of objects. Inactive when options is not bound or its first item is not an object.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when options is not bound (boundProps.options is falsy) or the first option is not an object.

properties.name: valueField
properties.description: Object property path to use as the value when options are bound to a collection of objects. Only relevant when options is a bound array of objects. Inactive when options is not bound or its first item is not an object.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when options is not bound (boundProps.options is falsy) or the first option is not an object.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the radio group is required for form validation.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: Layout direction of the radio buttons. 'column' for vertical layout, 'row' for horizontal layout.

properties.name: rowGap
properties.description: Vertical gap between radio button rows. Supports px, %, em, and rem units.

properties.name: columnGap
properties.description: Horizontal gap between radio button columns. Supports px, %, em, and rem units.

properties.name: justifyContent
properties.description: Flexbox justify-content alignment for the radio group container.

properties.name: alignItems
properties.description: Flexbox align-items alignment for the radio group container.

properties.name: flexWrap
properties.description: Whether radio buttons should wrap to the next line when in horizontal (row) direction. Only applies when direction is 'row'. Inactive when direction is not 'row'.

events.name: change
events.description: Triggered when the user selects a different radio option.

events.name: initValueChange
events.description: Triggered when the initial value is changed programmatically (e.g., via binding update).

slots.name: choicesElement
slots.description: ww-text element used as radio label. Text will be set using the 'label' configuration of the option.

variables.name: value
variables.description: The currently selected radio button value.

specifications: The value property sets the initial selected option and should match one of the option values exactly.
When binding dynamic data to options, use labelField and valueField to map object properties.
Use inside a ww-form-container when collecting form submissions; set a unique fieldName-equivalent via the surrounding form configuration.
flexWrap only applies when direction is set to 'row'.
Use ww-input-radio for mutually exclusive single-choice selection; use ww-input-checkbox for multi-select scenarios.
